templateMeta.name: Content Template
templateMeta.description: Generalized content artifact producer. Takes any combination of sources (URLs, text, references), a lookback period, and a content brief, then produces a labeled content artifact. Delegation is encouraged for parallel source coverage as long as all delegated branches converge.

name.description: Human label for this content run (e.g., 'Weekly AI Digest', 'Market Brief')

sources.description: Things to check for content — URLs, text, references. The agent decides how to use each.

lookbackPeriod.description: How far back to look for content (e.g., '7 days', '24 hours', '30 days')

contentBrief.description: What angle or purpose the content should serve — the substance guidance

formatBrief.description: Style and formatting guidance — tone (formal/casual), structure (e.g., '3 paragraphs'), length preferences, voice

formatRules.description: Machine-checkable formatting constraints. Applied in addition to formatBrief when provided.

fields.name: sourcesChecked
fields.description: Number of sources that were checked for content

fields.name: sourcesCited
fields.description: Number of sources actually cited in the output

fields.name: sourceEvidence
fields.description: Per-source evidence entries: source, method, artifactCid, citationsUsed, status

fields.name: formatCompliance
fields.description: Formatting compliance details: wordCount, structure, requiredSectionsPresent, constraintsMet

invariants.condition: You achieve complete source coverage across {{sources}} within {{lookbackPeriod}} relative to {{currentTimestamp}}. Delegation is desirable when it improves quality or speed. If you delegate, each child job must have a clear source scope, and all delegated branches must converge to terminal completion before final synthesis. For each source, produce evidence with source, method used (web_fetch/google_web_search/child), artifactCid, citationsUsed, and status.
invariants.assessment: Every source is covered and represented in sourceEvidence. Coverage can be direct or delegated, but no source is dropped. If child jobs are used, they converge and produce usable artifacts. sourcesChecked equals the number of input sources.

do: Delegate URL-heavy sources to child jobs and keep topic synthesis in the parent
Research directly when delegation overhead is unnecessary
Return a sourceEvidence entry for every source with artifact CID and status
dont: Dispatch child jobs without clear source ownership
Finalize output while delegated branches are still unresolved

invariants.condition: You synthesize findings into one coherent content artifact following {{contentBrief}} and {{formatBrief}}. The final compiled artifact MUST be created with type='TEMPLATE_OUTPUT' in create_artifact to mark it as the workstream's primary deliverable. If formatRules are provided, treat them as strict constraints (minWords/maxWords/primaryStructure/requiredSections/executiveSummarySentences/requiredCitations). You must return structured result fields for contentBody, sourcesChecked, sourcesCited, sourceEvidence, and formatCompliance.
invariants.assessment: The output is original synthesis organized by theme, cites sources, and matches requested structure/tone. formatCompliance reports wordCount, structure, required section coverage, and constraintsMet. sourcesCited satisfies citation targets when specified.

do: Connect findings across sources to surface patterns or insights
Cite specific sources when making claims
Organize content by theme, not by source
If formatRules.maxWords is set, keep content within that limit
If formatRules.requiredSections is set, include each named section
Call create_artifact with type='TEMPLATE_OUTPUT' for the final compiled article
dont: Return prose when primaryStructure requires bullets

invariants.assessment: Rate the output 0–100: Relevance to brief (25%) — does the content address the stated purpose? Source coverage (25%) — were the provided sources meaningfully used? Synthesis quality (25%) — is this more than the sum of its parts? Readability (25%) — is it clear, well-structured, and appropriate for the audience?
